digest.top_line: EORTC 22922/10925 20-year data extend durable IM-MSC irradiation benefit in stage I-III breast cancer, now including a pN0 subgroup — resolving a historically borderline survival signal.
digest.tldr: Breast cancer dominated ESTRO Day 2 with three landmark randomised trials at 10-20 year follow-up, collectively de-risking partial-field, dose-escalated SIB, and regional nodal irradiation strategies in early disease. PRIME addresses the open feasibility question of pelvic-nodal SBRT in high-risk prostate cancer, and OLIGOCARE (n~2500) delivers the largest prospective SABR registry dataset with a histology-stratified local failure signal.

sites.intro: Four randomised trials spanning regional nodal irradiation, dose-escalated SIB, partial-field RT, and oligometastatic MDT — anchored by 10-20 year follow-up durations.

studies.name: EORTC 22922/10925 — 20-year results
studies.tldr: Twenty-year EORTC 22922 data extend OS and DFS benefit from IM-MSC irradiation, with a new pN0 subgroup analysis.
details: No effect size in source tweet; images not accessible for extraction.
Prior 10-year data (Poortmans et al., NEJM 2015) showed borderline OS benefit and significant BCSS/DFS improvement; 20-year maturity likely resolves the OS signal.
Extension to pN0 subgroup is the clinically significant new analysis — IM-MSC RT benefit in node-negative, medially located tumors was previously undefined.
Era of enrollment (1996-2004) predates aromatase inhibitors and CDK4/6 inhibitors; absolute RT benefit may be attenuated in contemporary luminal patients on extended ET.
Open-label randomised design; competing mortality and intervening systemic therapy changes complicate direct causal attribution at 20 years.

studies.name: IMPORT HIGH — 10-year results
studies.tldr: Ten-year IMPORT HIGH results mature the dose-escalated simultaneous integrated boost signal in early breast cancer; tweet text truncated.
details: No effect size in tweet text; tweet is cut off mid-sentence.
Images (tweet 9, images 1-4) not accessible.
Trial (ISRCTN47437448) previously showed that higher SIB doses (48 Gy, 53 Gy vs 40 Gy control, all in 15#) reduced local recurrence without significantly increasing late effects at 5 years.
Ten-year maturity is the critical endpoint for late fibrosis, which was dose-dependent in 5-year data — highest SIB arm (53 Gy) had a signal worth following.
UK framing: IMPORT HIGH SIB data must now be interpreted alongside FAST-Forward (5#) — informs whether dose escalation adds value within abbreviated fractionation.

studies.name: APBI-IMRT Florence Phase III — 15-year results
studies.tldr: Fifteen-year APBI-IMRT Florence data assess durability of APBI non-inferiority vs whole-breast irradiation in selected early breast cancer.
details: No effect size in tweet text; images not accessible.
Trial previously demonstrated APBI non-inferiority for 5-year local relapse-free survival in node-negative, ER+ early breast cancer using IMRT-based external beam.
Fifteen years is the critical horizon for late hormone receptor-positive relapse — any LR curve divergence at this point would undermine the non-inferiority conclusion.
IMRT-based APBI (external beam) is methodologically distinct from GEC-ESTRO brachytherapy-based APBI; non-inferiority boundaries are not directly transferable.

Tweet 8 commentary ('suboptimal adherence to ET' affecting local control) is a relevant confound: luminal disease with non-adherent ET inflates LR risk independent of RT volume, potentially biasing APBI vs WBI comparison.
If non-inferiority is preserved at 15 years, this constitutes the longest APBI validation in a prospective randomised trial — clinically practice-changing for patient selection.

studies.name: OLIGOMA trial — metastases-directed treatment in oligometastatic breast cancer
studies.tldr: OLIGOMA (ARO-2021-09, NCT04495309) reports MDT outcomes in oligometastatic breast cancer; no effect size in source tweet.
details: No effect size or primary endpoint result in source tweet; images not accessible.
German ARO-sponsored prospective trial; design (randomised vs registry) not specified in tweet text.

Breast-specific oligometastatic MDT data are limited: NRG-BR002 (phase II, n=125) showed non-significant PFS improvement with SBRT vs standard care — OLIGOMA adds a disease-specific prospective dataset.
Receptor subtype breakdown is critical: ER+ de novo oligometastatic disease (systemic options include CDK4/6i) differs fundamentally from TNBC in expected RT benefit magnitude.
Without randomised design confirmation, selection bias toward favorable oligometastatic phenotype remains the principal methodological concern.

open_questions: EORTC 22922: what is the absolute OS benefit at 20 years in pN0 patients, and how does it compare to the borderline 10-year signal?
IMPORT HIGH: does 10-year late fibrosis data limit the highest SIB dose arm (53 Gy) or confirm its safety?
APBI-Florence: do 15-year LR curves remain parallel (non-inferiority preserved) or diverge — particularly in patients with documented ET non-adherence?
OLIGOMA: randomised or registry design, and is there a receptor-subtype-stratified subgroup analysis?

sites.intro: PRIME directly tests whether established prostate SBRT efficacy can be extended to simultaneous pelvic nodal irradiation in biologically aggressive disease on ADT.

studies.name: PRIME trial — pelvic-nodal SBRT vs moderate hypofractionation in high-risk prostate cancer
studies.tldr: PRIME compares SBRT (36.25 Gy/5#) vs moderate hypofractionation (~68 Gy/25#), both with pelvic nodal RT and ADT, in aggressive prostate cancer.
details: Moderate hypofractionation: ~68 Gy/25#/5 weeks; SBRT: 36.25 Gy/5#/1-2 weeks; both arms include pelvic nodal coverage.
No primary endpoint result or toxicity data in source tweet; images (tweet 4, images 13-14) not accessible.
HYPO RT-PC established extreme hypofractionation non-inferiority for prostate-only RT — PRIME extends this to simultaneous pelvic nodal SBRT, a meaningfully higher bowel-toxicity scenario.
PEACE II (tweet 1 context): established WPRT + 3y ADT benefit over prostate-only RT in high-risk disease — PRIME tests whether that nodal benefit is deliverable in 5 fractions.
POP RT (tweet 1): 2y ADT + WPRT comparator — ADT duration and field size interact; PRIME results require ADT duration context for valid benchmarking.
Principal safety concern: rectal and bowel dose constraints for large-field pelvic SBRT at 36.25 Gy equivalent are not established in phase III data — this is the main gap PRIME addresses.

open_questions: Primary endpoint of PRIME — biochemical failure, MFS, or OS — and what is the median follow-up reported?
Acute and late GI/GU toxicity rates with pelvic-nodal SBRT vs moderate hypofractionation?
ADT duration in PRIME arms — does it align with PEACE II (3y) or POP RT (2y) to allow indirect comparison?

sites.intro: OLIGOCARE delivers the largest prospective SABR registry dataset to date, with a histology-stratified failure signal and a dosimetric quality correlate.

studies.name: OLIGOCARE — EORTC e2irradiate prospective SABR registry for oligometastases
studies.tldr: OLIGOCARE (n~2500 pts, ~3500 mets): SABR local failure 5% at 1y and 11% at 3y; CRC carries higher progression risk; minimum PTV dose correlates with LC.
details: Local failure: 5% at 1 year, 11% at 3 years across all histologies.
Colorectal cancer subgroup: higher local/systemic progression risk vs other histologies — consistent with radiobiologic resistance and liver/lung metastatic propensity in CRC.
Minimum PTV dose (cold-spot dosimetry) correlates with local control — a dosimetric quality-assurance signal with direct planning optimization implications.
RCC SBRT (tweet 6): ~100% LC at 5 years reported via commentary link — sharply contrasts with CRC data and supports histology-stratified SABR eligibility frameworks.
Registry design without randomised comparator; performance-status selection bias and variable systemic therapy backgrounds limit efficacy inference; toxicity data not reported in source tweets.
At ~2500 patients, OLIGOCARE substantially exceeds prior randomised oligomets trials (SABR-COMET ~99 pts, ORIOLE ~54 pts) in sample size, though design differences preclude direct comparison.

open_questions: Is the RCC 100% LC at 5 years (tweet 6) from the OLIGOCARE RCC subgroup or a separate ESTRO26 abstract?
Does the minimum PTV dose threshold define a plan adequacy standard that should be prospectively enforced in future SABR trials?
OS or PFS by histology from OLIGOCARE — does the CRC local failure signal translate into inferior survival vs radiosensitive histologies?
